user_request: Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks recovery of Rs. 20,00,000 paid as advance for a business transaction that failed due to the Defendant's default. The suit claims total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, seeking refund with interest and costs.

slots.value: Recovery of advance with interest and costs

assumptions: Jurisdiction lies in the specified District Court based on cause of action or defendant residence
Suit is within the limitation period

must be read subject to its provisions. Under s 8 of Suits Valuation Act, 1887 the valuation for court fees and 
the valuation for jurisdiction in certain suits is the same. Under s 7 cl (iv)(f) of the Court Fees Act, 1870, the 
valuation for purposes of court fees in a suit for accounts lies in the discretion of the plaintiff. Therefore, in a suit 
for accounts, the plaintiffs valuation in the plaint fixes the jurisdiction of the court. 2 In a suit for unsettled 
accounts, fixed court fee is payable and for jurisdictional purposes, such a suit can be valued in terms of 
valuation as fixed by the plaintiff. 3 In a suit for partition, the value for the purpose of jurisdiction is the value of 
the share claimed by the plaintiff and not the value of the entire estate in which the partition is sought. 4 In a suit 
for recovery of property from the tenant whose tenancy has been terminated, the valuation of suit for 
jurisdictional purposes should be on the basis of annual rent or profit. Suit so valued along with the claim of 
damage exceeding pecuniary jurisdiction of the court is liable to be returned for prosecution before the proper 
court.5

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid as Advance with Interest and Costs against {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}
draft_artifacts.text:
1. Plaintiff and Defendant:
   The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}} and carrying on business at {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_ADDRESS}}, files this suit against {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} (hereinafter referred to as the "Defendant"), his place of business at {{DEFENDANT_BUSINESS_ADDRESS}}, for recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) paid as advance for a business transaction which has wholly failed due to the default of the Defendant.

2. Nature of Suit and Relief Sought:
   This is a suit for the recovery of money on the ground of total failure of consideration, under the principles applicable to contracts; the Plaintiff prays for refund of Rs. 20,00,000/- together with interest thereon and costs of the suit.

3. Facts constituting the cause of action:
   3.1 On {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant as an advance for a contemplated business transaction. The amount was paid in good faith with the expectation that the transaction would be completed in accordance with the agreed terms.
   3.2 The Defendant defaulted in performance, and as a consequence the entire consideration paid by the Plaintiff has become totally immaterial and worthless in the sense that there has been a total failure of consideration.
   3.3 The Plaintiff has demanded refund of the amount paid, but the Defendant has failed and refused to refund the same, despite requests.

4. Cause of Action (accrual, continuing nature and setting out accrual date):
   4.1 The cause of action arose on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} on account of the Defendant's default and refusal to refund the sum of Rs. 20,00,000/-, and the Plaintiff remains ready and entitled to recover the same. 
   4.2 The cause of action is of a continuing nature, as the amount remains unpaid to date and the Plaintiff is entitled to a refund now and in the future, subject to such interest and costs as may be awarded by this Court.
   4.3 Accrual event: The right to sue accrued on the date of default/refusal to refund and the Plaintiff has issued such demand as may be required, but the Defendant still fails to refund.

5. Valuation and Court Fee:
   5.1 The value of the suit for purposes of jurisdiction and court fee is Rs. 20,00,000/-. 
   5.2 Court fee payable on the plaint is Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (to be computed per the applicable Court Fee schedule); the exact amount is to be verified at the time of filing.

6. Interest:
   6.1 Interest, if any, on the amount refunded shall be calculated at {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} until realization, as permitted by law and as may be directed by this Hon'ble Court.

7. Costs:
   7.1 The Plaintiff prays for costs of the suit.

8. Verification and Affidavit:
   I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff in the above matter, do hereby declare and affirm as under:
   (a) I have read the contents of this plaint and verify that the same are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief; 
   (b) The facts stated herein are within my personal knowledge and belief; 
   (c) I have not suppressed any material fact; 
   Verification: Verified at {{PLACE}} on this {{VERIFICATION_DATE}} day of {{MONTH}}, {{YEAR}}.

9. List of Documents / Enclosures (Annexures):
   - Annexure A: Proof of payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- (advance payment).
   - Annexure B: Any communications or correspondence exchanged in relation to the transaction.
   - Annexure C: Any other documents in support of the claim.

10. Place, Date and Court:
   File in the District Court at {{PLACE}} within the territorial jurisdiction where the Defendant resides or carries on business, for a decision on the claim for recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- with interest and costs.

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid as Advance with Interest and Costs against {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{PLACE}}

Civil Suit No. ____ of {{YEAR}}

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
S/o/D/o/W/o __________,
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}},
Carrying on business at: {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_ADDRESS}}.                                         ...Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
S/o/D/o/W/o __________,
Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}},
Carrying on business at: {{DEFENDANT_BUSINESS_ADDRESS}}.                                         ...Defendant

PLAINT
(Suit for recovery of money—refund of advance upon total failure of consideration, with interest and costs)

1. Parties
1.1 The Plaintiff is an adult, competent to sue, and is engaged in business.
1.2 The Defendant is an adult, competent to be sued, and carries on business as stated above.

2. Jurisdiction
2.1 This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the Defendant resides/carries on business within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court and/or material part of the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.
2.2 This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain the present suit as the suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only), which is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.

3. Facts
3.1 On/around {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a business arrangement/transaction ("the Transaction") whereby the Defendant agreed to perform/arrange/execute {{TRANSACTION_BRIEF_DESCRIPTION}} on or before {{PERFORMANCE_DUE_DATE}} on the agreed terms.
3.2 Pursuant to the aforesaid Transaction and as part consideration/advance, the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) to the Defendant on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} through {{MODE_OF_PAYMENT}}. The proof of such payment is filed as Annexure A.
3.3 The Defendant failed to perform and/or fulfil his obligations under the Transaction by {{PERFORMANCE_DUE_DATE}} and thereby committed default.
3.4 Due to the Defendant’s default, the Transaction failed and the Plaintiff received no benefit/consideration whatsoever against the advance paid. Accordingly, there has been a total failure of consideration.
3.5 The Plaintiff called upon the Defendant to refund the advance amount of Rs. 20,00,000/-, inter alia by communications dated {{DEMAND_DATE}}. Copies of relevant communications are filed as Annexure B.
3.6 Despite demands, the Defendant has failed and neglected and/or refused to refund the said amount. The Defendant’s refusal/inaction continues and the amount remains due and payable to the Plaintiff.

4. Legal basis (Total failure of consideration / restitution)
4.1 The Plaintiff submits that upon the Transaction becoming ineffective/failed/void due to the Defendant’s default and there being total failure of consideration, the Defendant is bound in law to restore/repay the benefit received, i.e., the advance amount of Rs. 20,00,000/-.
4.2 The Plaintiff specifically relies upon the restitutionary principle embodied in {{SECTION_65_INDIAN_CONTRACT_ACT_1872}} (to be verified), and submits that the Defendant is liable to refund the advance received by him when the underlying consideration has totally failed.

5. Cause of Action (accrual date and continuing/subsisting nature)
5.1 The cause of action first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} when the Defendant failed to perform his obligations by the agreed date {{PERFORMANCE_DUE_DATE}}.
5.2 The cause of action further arose on {{DEMAND_DATE}} when the Plaintiff demanded refund and on {{REFUSAL_DATE}} when the Defendant refused/failed to refund.
5.3 The cause of action continues to subsist as on the date of filing of this suit as the Defendant continues to wrongfully retain the Plaintiff’s money and has not refunded the amount.

6. Limitation
6.1 The suit is within limitation as the cause of action first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} / {{REFUSAL_DATE}} (as applicable) and the suit is being filed within the prescribed period computed therefrom.

7. Valuation and Court Fee
7.1 For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only).
7.2 The requisite court fee computed on the above suit valuation as per the applicable Court Fees Act / State amendment is affixed/shall be affixed. The court fee amount is {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (to be computed and verified at filing).

8. Interest
8.1 The Plaintiff claims interest on Rs. 20,00,000/- at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{INTEREST_FROM_DATE}} (being the date of default/refusal, as applicable) till realization, and/or such other rate and period as this Hon’ble Court deems fit in the facts and circumstances.

9. Costs
9.1 The Plaintiff prays for costs of the suit.

10. Prayer / Reliefs Sought
In view of the facts stated above, the Plaintiff respectfully prays that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to:
(a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only);
(b) Award interest on the said sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{INTEREST_FROM_DATE}} till the date of realization (or such other rate/period as this Hon’ble Court deems fit);
(c) Award costs of the suit; and
(d) Grant such other and further reliefs as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

11. List of Documents / Enclosures (Annexures)
Annexure A: Proof of payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- (advance payment) dated {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}.
Annexure B: Correspondence/demand(s) for refund and/or Defendant’s reply/refusal, including communications dated {{DEMAND_DATE}} / {{REFUSAL_DATE}}.
Annexure C: Any other documents relied upon by the Plaintiff.

12. Verification
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 11 are true and correct to my personal knowledge, except those stated on information and belief (if any), which I believe to be true, and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{PLACE}} on this {{VERIFICATION_DATE}} day of {{MONTH}}, {{YEAR}}.

_________________________
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
Plaintiff
